NKJV.1: Oh, that you would bear with me in a little folly—and indeed you do bear with me.
NKJV.2: For I am jealous for you with godly jealousy. For I have betrothed you to one husband, that I may present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
NKJV.3: But I fear, lest somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve by his craftiness, so your minds may be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.
NKJV.4: For if he who comes preaches another Jesus whom we have not preached, or if you receive a different spirit which you have not received, or a different gospel which you have not accepted—you may well put up with it!
NKJV.5: For I consider that I am not at all inferior to the most eminent apostles.
NKJV.6: Even though I am untrained in speech, yet I am not in knowledge. But we have been thoroughly manifested among you in all things.
NKJV.7: Did I commit sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge?
NKJV.8: I robbed other churches, taking wages from them to minister to you.
NKJV.9: And when I was present with you, and in need, I was a burden to no one, for what I lacked the brethren who came from Macedonia supplied. And in everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and so I will keep myself.
NKJV.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, no one shall stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
NKJV.11: Why? Because I do not love you? God knows!
NKJV.12: But what I do, I will also continue to do, that I may cut off the opportunity from those who desire an opportunity to be regarded just as we are in the things of which they boast.
NKJV.13: For such are false apostles, deceitful workers, transforming themselves into apostles of Christ.
NKJV.14: And no wonder! For Satan himself transforms himself into an angel of light.
NKJV.15: Therefore it is no great thing if his ministers also transform themselves into ministers of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.
NKJV.16: I say again, let no one think me a fool. If otherwise, at least receive me as a fool, that I also may boast a little.
NKJV.17: What I speak, I speak not according to the Lord, but as it were, foolishly, in this confidence of boasting.
NKJV.18: Seeing that many boast according to the flesh, I also will boast.
NKJV.19: For you put up with fools gladly, since you yourselves are wise!
NKJV.20: For you put up with it if one brings you into bondage, if one devours you, if one takes from you, if one exalts himself, if one strikes you on the face.
NKJV.21: To our shame I say that we were too weak for that! But in whatever anyone is bold—I speak foolishly—I am bold also.
NKJV.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the seed of Abraham? So am I.
NKJV.23: Are they ministers of Christ?—I speak as a fool—I am more: in labors more abundant, in stripes above measure, in prisons more frequently, in deaths often.
NKJV.24: From the Jews five times I received forty stripes minus one.
NKJV.25: Three times I was beaten with rods; once I was stoned; three times I was shipwrecked; a night and a day I have been in the deep;
NKJV.26: in journeys often, in perils of waters, in perils of robbers, in perils of my own countrymen, in perils of the Gentiles, in perils in the city, in perils in the wilderness, in perils in the sea, in perils among false brethren;
NKJV.27: in weariness and toil, in sleeplessness often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, in cold and nakedness—
NKJV.28: besides the other things, what comes upon me daily: my deep concern for all the churches.
NKJV.29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I do not burn with indignation?
NKJV.30: If I must boast, I will boast in the things which concern my infirmity.
NKJV.31: The God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
NKJV.32: In Damascus the governor, under Aretas the king, was guarding the city of the Damascenes with a garrison, desiring to arrest me;
NKJV.33: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and escaped from his hands.

ESV.1: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
ESV.2: For I feel a divine jealousy for you, since I betrothed you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
ESV.3: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by his cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
ESV.4: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or if you accept a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it readily enough.
ESV.5: Indeed, I consider that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
ESV.6: Even if I am unskilled in speaking, I am not so in knowledge; indeed, in every way we have made this plain to you in all things.
ESV.7: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached God's gospel to you free of charge?
ESV.8: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
ESV.9: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied my need. So I refrained and will refrain from burdening you in any way.
ESV.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
ESV.11: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
ESV.12: And what I am doing I will continue to do, in order to undermine the claim of those who would like to claim that in their boasted mission they work on the same terms as we do.
ESV.13: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workmen, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
ESV.14: And no wonder, for even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
ESV.15: So it is no surprise if his servants, also, disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will correspond to their deeds.
ESV.16: I repeat, let no one think me foolish. But even if you do, accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
ESV.17: What I am saying with this boastful confidence, I say not as the Lord would  but as a fool.
ESV.18: Since many boast according to the flesh, I too will boast.
ESV.19: For you gladly bear with fools, being wise yourselves!
ESV.20: For you bear it if someone makes slaves of you, or devours you, or takes advantage of you, or puts on airs, or strikes you in the face.
ESV.21: To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone else dares to boast of — I am speaking as a fool — I also dare to boast of that.
ESV.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they offspring of Abraham? So am I.
ESV.23: Are they servants of Christ? I am a better one — I am talking like a madman — with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, with countless beatings, and often near death.
ESV.24: Five times I received at the hands of the Jews the forty lashes less one.
ESV.25: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked; a night and a day I was adrift at sea;
ESV.26: on frequent journeys, in danger from rivers, danger from robbers, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from false brothers;
ESV.27: in toil and hardship, through many a sleepless night, in hunger and thirst, often without food,  in cold and exposure.
ESV.28: And, apart from other things, there is the daily pressure on me of my anxiety for all the churches.
ESV.29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to fall, and I am not indignant?
ESV.30: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
ESV.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, he who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
ESV.32: At Damascus, the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to seize me,
ESV.33: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall and escaped his hands.

NASB.1: I wish that you would bear with me in a little foolishness; but indeed you are bearing with me.
NASB.2: For I am jealous for you with a godly jealousy; for I betrothed you to one husband, so that to Christ I might present you [as] a pure virgin.
NASB.3: But I am afraid that, as the serpent deceived Eve by his craftiness, your minds will be led astray from the simplicity and purity [of devotion] to Christ.
NASB.4: For if one comes and preaches another Jesus whom we have not preached, or you receive a different spirit which you have not received, or a different gospel which you have not accepted, you bear [this] beautifully.
NASB.5: For I consider myself not in the least inferior to the most eminent apostles.
NASB.6: But even if I am unskilled in speech, yet I am not [so] in knowledge; in fact, in every way we have made [this] evident to you in all things.
NASB.7: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I preached the gospel of God to you without charge?
NASB.8: I robbed other churches by taking wages [from] [them] to serve you;
NASB.9: and when I was present with you and was in need, I was not a burden to anyone; for when the brethren came from Macedonia they fully supplied my need, and in everything I kept myself from being a burden to you, and will continue to do so.
NASB.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be stopped in the regions of Achaia.
NASB.11: Why? Because I do not love you? God knows [I do]!
NASB.12: But what I am doing I will continue to do, so that I may cut off opportunity from those who desire an opportunity to be regarded just as we are in the matter about which they are boasting.
NASB.13: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
NASB.14: No wonder, for even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
NASB.15: Therefore it is not surprising if his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their deeds.
NASB.16: Again I say, let no one think me foolish; but if [you do], receive me even as foolish, so that I also may boast a little.
NASB.17: What I am saying, I am not saying as the Lord would, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting.
NASB.18: Since many boast according to the flesh, I will boast also.
NASB.19: For you, being [so] wise, tolerate the foolish gladly.
NASB.20: For you tolerate it if anyone enslaves you, anyone devours you, anyone takes advantage of you, anyone exalts himself, anyone hits you in the face.
NASB.21: To [my] shame I [must] say that we have been weak [by comparison]. But in whatever respect anyone [else] is bold-- I speak in foolishness-- I am just as bold myself.
NASB.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
NASB.23: Are they servants of Christ?-- I speak as if insane-- I more so; in far more labors, in far more imprisonments, beaten times without number, often in danger of death.
NASB.24: Five times I received from the Jews thirty-nine [lashes].
NASB.25: Three times I was beaten with rods, once I was stoned, three times I was shipwrecked, a night and a day I have spent in the deep.
NASB.26: [I have been] on frequent journeys, in dangers from rivers, dangers from robbers, dangers from [my] countrymen, dangers from the Gentiles, dangers in the city, dangers in the wilderness, dangers on the sea, dangers among false brethren;
NASB.27: [I have been] in labor and hardship, through many sleepless nights, in hunger and thirst, often without food, in cold and exposure.
NASB.28: Apart from [such] external things, there is the daily pressure on me [of] concern for all the churches.
NASB.29: Who is weak without my being weak? Who is led into sin without my intense concern?
NASB.30: If I have to boast, I will boast of what pertains to my weakness.
NASB.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, He who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
NASB.32: In Damascus the ethnarch under Aretas the king was guarding the city of the Damascenes in order to seize me,
NASB.33: and I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and [so] escaped his hands.

NLT.1: I hope you will put up with a little more of my foolishness. Please bear with me.
NLT.2: For I am jealous for you with the jealousy of God himself. I promised you as a pure bride to one husband — Christ.
NLT.3: But I fear that somehow your pure and undivided devotion to Christ will be corrupted, just as Eve was deceived by the cunning ways of the serpent.
NLT.4: You happily put up with whatever anyone tells you, even if they preach a different Jesus than the one we preach, or a different kind of Spirit than the one you received, or a different kind of gospel than the one you believed.
NLT.5: But I don’t consider myself inferior in any way to these “super apostles” who teach such things.
NLT.6: I may be unskilled as a speaker, but I’m not lacking in knowledge. We have made this clear to you in every possible way.
NLT.7: Was I wrong when I humbled myself and honored you by preaching God’s Good News to you without expecting anything in return?
NLT.8: I “robbed” other churches by accepting their contributions so I could serve you at no cost.
NLT.9: And when I was with you and didn’t have enough to live on, I did not become a financial burden to anyone. For the brothers who came from Macedonia brought me all that I needed. I have never been a burden to you, and I never will be.
NLT.10: As surely as the truth of Christ is in me, no one in all of Greece will ever stop me from boasting about this.
NLT.11: Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows that I do.
NLT.12: But I will continue doing what I have always done. This will undercut those who are looking for an opportunity to boast that their work is just like ours.
NLT.13: These people are false apostles. They are deceitful workers who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
NLT.14: But I am not surprised! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
NLT.15: So it is no wonder that his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. In the end they will get the punishment their wicked deeds deserve.
NLT.16: Again I say, don’t think that I am a fool to talk like this. But even if you do, listen to me, as you would to a foolish person, while I also boast a little.
NLT.17: Such boasting is not from the Lord, but I am acting like a fool.
NLT.18: And since others boast about their human achievements, I will, too.
NLT.19: After all, you think you are so wise, but you enjoy putting up with fools!
NLT.20: You put up with it when someone enslaves you, takes everything you have, takes advantage of you, takes control of everything, and slaps you in the face.
NLT.21: I’m ashamed to say that we’ve been too “weak” to do that! But whatever they dare to boast about — I’m talking like a fool again — I dare to boast about it, too.
NLT.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
NLT.23: Are they servants of Christ? I know I sound like a madman, but I have served him far more! I have worked harder, been put in prison more often, been whipped times without number, and faced death again and again.
NLT.24: Five different times the Jewish leaders gave me thirty-nine lashes.
NLT.25: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked. Once I spent a whole night and a day adrift at sea.
NLT.26: I have traveled on many long journeys. I have faced danger from rivers and from robbers. I have faced danger from my own people, the Jews, as well as from the Gentiles. I have faced danger in the cities, in the deserts, and on the seas. And I have faced danger from men who claim to be believers but are not.
NLT.27: I have worked hard and long, enduring many sleepless nights. I have been hungry and thirsty and have often gone without food. I have shivered in the cold, without enough clothing to keep me warm.
NLT.28: Then, besides all this, I have the daily burden of my concern for all the churches.
NLT.29: Who is weak without my feeling that weakness? Who is led astray, and I do not burn with anger?
NLT.30: If I must boast, I would rather boast about the things that show how weak I am.
NLT.31: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, who is worthy of eternal praise, knows I am not lying.
NLT.32: When I was in Damascus, the governor under King Aretas kept guards at the city gates to catch me.
NLT.33: I had to be lowered in a basket through a window in the city wall to escape from him.

WEB.1: I wish that you would bear with me in a little foolishness, but indeed you do bear with me.
WEB.2: For I am jealous over you with a godly jealousy. For I married you to one husband, that I might present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
WEB.3: But I am afraid that somehow, as the serpent deceived Eve in his craftiness, so your minds might be corrupted from the simplicity that is in Christ.
WEB.4: For if he who comes preaches another Jesus, whom we did not preach, or if you receive a different spirit, which you did not receive, or a different “good news”, which you did not accept, you put up with that well enough.
WEB.5: For I reckon that I am not at all behind the very best apostles.
WEB.6: But though I am unskilled in speech, yet I am not unskilled in knowledge. No, in every way we have been revealed to you in all things.
WEB.7: Or did I commit a sin in humbling myself that you might be exalted, because I preached to you God’s Good News free of charge?
WEB.8: I robbed other assemblies, taking wages from them that I might serve you.
WEB.9: When I was present with you and was in need, I wasn’t a burden on anyone, for the brothers, when they came from Macedonia, supplied the measure of my need. In everything I kept myself from being burdensome to you, and I will continue to do so.
WEB.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, no one will stop me from this boasting in the regions of Achaia.
WEB.11: Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows.
WEB.12: But what I do, that I will do, that I may cut off occasion from them that desire an occasion, that in which they boast, they may be found even as we.
WEB.13: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workers, masquerading as Christ’s apostles.
WEB.14: And no wonder, for even Satan masquerades as an angel of light.
WEB.15: It is no great thing therefore if his servants also masquerade as servants of righteousness, whose end will be according to their works.
WEB.16: I say again, let no one think me foolish. But if so, yet receive me as foolish, that I also may boast a little.
WEB.17: That which I speak, I don’t speak according to the Lord, but as in foolishness, in this confidence of boasting.
WEB.18: Seeing that many boast after the flesh, I will also boast.
WEB.19: For you bear with the foolish gladly, being wise.
WEB.20: For you bear with a man, if he brings you into bondage, if he devours you, if he takes you captive, if he exalts himself, if he strikes you on the face.
WEB.21: I speak by way of disparagement, as though we had been weak. Yet in whatever way anyone is bold (I speak in foolishness), I am bold also.
WEB.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the offspring  of Abraham? So am I.
WEB.23: Are they servants of Christ? (I speak as one beside himself) I am more so; in labors more abundantly, in prisons more abundantly, in stripes above measure, in deaths often.
WEB.24: Five times from the Jews I received forty stripes minus one.
WEB.25: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I was stoned. Three times I suffered shipwreck. I have been a night and a day in the deep.
WEB.26: I have been in travels often, perils of rivers, perils of robbers, perils from my countrymen, perils from the Gentiles, perils in the city, perils in the wilderness, perils in the sea, perils among false brothers;
WEB.27: in labor and travail, in watchings often, in hunger and thirst, in fastings often, and in cold and nakedness.
WEB.28: Besides those things that are outside, there is that which presses on me daily, anxiety for all the assemblies.
WEB.29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is caused to stumble, and I don’t burn with indignation?
WEB.30: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that concern my weakness.
WEB.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ, he who is blessed forever more, knows that I don’t lie.
WEB.32: In Damascus the governor under King Aretas guarded the city of the Damascenes desiring to arrest me.
WEB.33: Through a window I was let down in a basket by the wall, and escaped his hands.

CSB17.1: I wish you would put up with a little foolishness <sup>ⓕ</sup> from me. Yes, do put up with me! <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.2: For I am jealous <sup>ⓖ</sup> for you with a godly jealousy, <sup>ⓗ</sup> because I have promised you in marriage to one husband — to present a pure <sup>ⓘ</sup> virgin to Christ.
CSB17.3: But I fear that, as the serpent <sup>ⓙ</sup> deceived <sup>ⓚ</sup> Eve <sup>ⓛ</sup> by his cunning, your minds may be seduced from a sincere and pure <sup>[2]</sup> devotion to Christ. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.4: For if a person comes and preaches another Jesus, <sup>ⓝ</sup> whom we did not preach, or you receive a different spirit, <sup>ⓐ</sup> which you had not received, or a different gospel, <sup>ⓑ</sup> which you had not accepted, you put up with it splendidly!
CSB17.5: Now I consider myself in no way inferior to those “super-apostles.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.6: Even if I am untrained in public speaking, <sup>ⓓ</sup> I am certainly not untrained in knowledge. <sup>ⓔ</sup> Indeed, we have in every way made that clear to you in everything.
CSB17.7: Or did I commit a sin by humbling <sup>ⓕ</sup> myself so that you might be exalted, <sup>ⓖ</sup> because I preached the gospel of God to you free of charge? <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.8: I robbed other churches by taking pay from them to minister to you.
CSB17.9: When I was present with you and in need, I did not burden anyone, since the brothers who came from Macedonia <sup>ⓘ</sup> supplied my needs. <sup>ⓙ</sup> I have kept myself, and will keep myself, from burdening you in any way.
CSB17.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be stopped <sup>[3]</sup> in the regions of Achaia. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.11: Why? Because I don’t love you? God knows I do!
CSB17.12: But I will continue to do what I am doing, in order to deny <sup>[4]</sup> an opportunity to those who want to be regarded as our equals in what they boast about.
CSB17.13: For such people are false apostles, <sup>ⓛ</sup> deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
CSB17.14: And no wonder! For Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
CSB17.15: So it is no great surprise if his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be according to their works. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.16: I repeat: Let no one consider me a fool. But if you do, at least accept me as a fool so that I can also boast a little.
CSB17.17: What I am saying in this matter <sup>[5]</sup> of boasting, I don’t speak as the Lord would, but as it were, foolishly.
CSB17.18: Since many boast according to the flesh, I will also boast.
CSB17.19: For you, being so wise, gladly put up with fools! <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.20: In fact, you put up with it if someone enslaves you, if someone exploits <sup>ⓐ</sup> you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone is arrogant toward you, if someone slaps <sup>ⓑ</sup> you in the face.
CSB17.21: I say this to our shame: We have been too weak for that! But in whatever anyone dares to boast — I am talking foolishly — I also dare:
CSB17.22: Are they Hebrews? <sup>ⓒ</sup> So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they the descendants of Abraham? <sup>ⓓ</sup> So am I.
CSB17.23: Are they servants of Christ? I’m talking like a madman — I’m a better one: with far more labors, many more imprisonments, far worse beatings, many times near death.
CSB17.24: Five times I received the forty lashes minus one from the Jews. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.25: Three times I was beaten with rods. <sup>ⓕ</sup> Once I received a stoning. <sup>ⓖ</sup> Three times I was shipwrecked. <sup>ⓗ</sup> I have spent a night and a day in the open sea.
CSB17.26: On frequent journeys, I faced dangers from rivers, dangers from robbers, <sup>ⓘ</sup> dangers from my own people, <sup>ⓙ</sup> dangers from Gentiles, dangers in the city, dangers in the wilderness, dangers at sea, and dangers among false brothers;
CSB17.27: toil and hardship, many sleepless nights, hunger and thirst, often without food, cold, and without clothing. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.28: Not to mention <sup>[6]</sup> other things, there is the daily pressure on me: my concern for all the churches.
CSB17.29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and I do not burn with indignation?
CSB17.30: If boasting is necessary, I will boast about my weaknesses.
CSB17.31: The God and Father <sup>ⓜ</sup> of the Lord Jesus, who is blessed forever, knows I am not lying.
CSB17.32: In Damascus, <sup>ⓝ</sup> a ruler <sup>[7]</sup> under King Aretas guarded the city of Damascus in order to arrest me.
CSB17.33: So I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall and escaped from his hands. <sup>ⓐ</sup>

AMP.1: I wish you would bear with me [while I indulge] in a little foolishness; but indeed you are bearing with me [as you read this].
AMP.2: I am jealous for you with a godly jealousy because I have promised you to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
AMP.3: But I am afraid that, even as the serpent beguiled Eve by his cunning, your minds may be corrupted and led away from the simplicity of [your sincere and] pure devotion to Christ.
AMP.4: For [you seem willing to allow it] if one comes and preaches another Jesus whom we have not preached, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or a different gospel from the one you accepted. You tolerate all this beautifully [welcoming the deception].
AMP.5: Yet I consider myself in no way inferior to the [so-called] super-apostles.
AMP.6: But even if I am unskilled in speaking, yet I am not [untrained] in knowledge [I know what I am talking about]; but we have made this evident to you in every way, in all things.
AMP.7: Or did I [perhaps] sin by humbling myself so that you might be exalted and honored, because I preached God's gospel to you free of charge?
AMP.8: I robbed other churches by accepting [more than their share of] financial support for my ministry to you.
AMP.9: And when I was with you and ran short [financially], I did not burden any of you; for what I needed was fully supplied by the brothers (Silas and Timothy) who came from Macedonia (the church at Philippi). So I kept myself from being a burden to you in any way, and will continue to do so.
AMP.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, my boast [of independence] will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia (southern Greece).
AMP.11: Why? Because I do not love you [or wish you well, or have regard for your welfare]? God knows [that I do]!
AMP.12: But what I am doing I will keep doing, [for I am determined to keep this independence] in order to cut off the claim of those who want an opportunity to be regarded just as we are in the things they brag about.
AMP.13: For such men are counterfeit apostles, deceitful workers, masquerading as apostles of Christ.
AMP.14: And no wonder, since Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light.
AMP.15: So it is no great surprise if his servants also masquerade as servants of righteousness, but their end will correspond with their deeds.
AMP.16: I repeat then, let no one think that I am foolish; but even if you do, at least accept me as foolish, so that I too may boast a little.
AMP.17: What I say in this confident boasting, I say not as the Lord would [with His authority], but foolishly.
AMP.18: Since many boast [of worldly things and brag] about human accomplishments, I will boast too.
AMP.19: For you, being so wise, gladly tolerate and accept the foolish [like me]!
AMP.20: For you tolerate it if anyone makes you his slave; or devours you and your possessions; or takes advantage of you; or acts presumptuously; or hits you in the face.
AMP.21:
To my shame, I must say, we have been too weak [in comparison to those pseudo-apostles who take advantage of you].
But in whatever anyone else dares to boast—I am speaking foolishly—I also dare to boast.
AMP.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
AMP.23: Are they [self-proclaimed] servants of Christ?—I am speaking as if I were out of my mind—I am more so [for I exceed them]; with far more labors, with far more imprisonments, beaten times without number, and often in danger of death.
AMP.24: Five times I received from the Jews thirty-nine lashes.
AMP.25: Three times I was beaten with rods, once I was stoned. Three times I was shipwrecked, a night and a day I have spent adrift on the sea;
AMP.26: many times on journeys, [exposed to] danger from rivers, danger from bandits, danger from my own countrymen, danger from the Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger on the sea, danger among those posing as believers;
AMP.27: in labor and hardship, often unable to sleep, in hunger and thirst, often [driven to] fasting [for lack of food], in cold and exposure [without adequate clothing].
AMP.28: Besides those external things, there is the daily [inescapable] pressure of my concern for all the churches.
AMP.29: Who is weak, and I do not feel [his] weakness? Who is made to sin, and I am not on fire [with sorrow and concern]?
AMP.30: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that reveal my weakness [the things by which I am made weak in the eyes of my opponents].
AMP.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, He who is blessed and to be praised forevermore, knows that I am not lying.
AMP.32: In Damascus the governor (ethnarch) under King Aretas guarded the city of Damascus in order to arrest me,
AMP.33: and I was [actually] let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and slipped through his fingers.

MSG.1: Will you put up with a little foolish aside from me? Please, just for a moment.
MSG.2: The thing that has me so upset is that I care about you so much—this is the passion of God burning inside me! I promised your hand in marriage to Christ, presented you as a pure virgin to her husband.
MSG.3: And now I'm afraid that exactly as the Snake seduced Eve with his smooth patter, you are being lured away from the simple purity of your love for Christ.
MSG.4: It seems that if someone shows up preaching quite another Jesus than we preached—different spirit, different message—you put up with him quite nicely.
MSG.5: But if you put up with these big-shot "apostles," why can't you put up with simple me? I'm as good as they are.
MSG.6: It's true that I don't have their voice, haven't mastered that smooth eloquence that impresses you so much. But when I do open my mouth, I at least know what I'm talking about. We haven't kept anything back. We let you in on everything.
MSG.7: I wonder, did I make a bad mistake in proclaiming God's Message to you without asking for something in return, serving you free of charge so that you wouldn't be inconvenienced by me?
MSG.8: It turns out that the other churches paid my way so that you could have a free ride.
MSG.9: Not once during the time I lived among you did anyone have to lift a finger to help me out. My needs were always supplied by the Christians from Macedonia province. I was careful never to be a burden to you, and I never will be, you can count on it.
MSG.10: With Christ as my witness, it's a point of honor with me, and I'm not going to keep it quiet just to protect you from what the neighbors will think.
MSG.11: It's not that I don't love you; God knows I do.
MSG.12: I'm just trying to keep things open and honest between us. And I'm not changing my position on this. I'd die before taking your money. I'm giving nobody grounds for lumping me in with those money-grubbing "preachers," vaunting themselves as something special.
MSG.13: They're a sorry bunch—pseudo-apostles, lying preachers, crooked workers—posing as Christ's agents but sham to the core.
MSG.14: And no wonder! Satan does it all the time, dressing up as a beautiful angel of light.
MSG.15: So it shouldn't surprise us when his servants masquerade as servants of God. But they're not getting by with anything. They'll pay for it in the end.
MSG.16: Let me come back to where I started—and don't hold it against me if I continue to sound a little foolish. Or if you'd rather, just accept that I am a fool and let me rant on a little.
MSG.17: I didn't learn this kind of talk from Christ.
MSG.18: Oh, no, it's a bad habit I picked up from the three-ring preachers that are so popular these days.
MSG.19: Since you sit there in the judgment seat observing all these shenanigans, you can afford to humor an occasional fool who happens along.
MSG.20: You have such admirable tolerance for impostors who rob your freedom, rip you off, steal you blind, put you down—even slap your face!
MSG.21: I shouldn't admit it to you, but our stomachs aren't strong enough to tolerate that kind of stuff. Since you admire the egomaniacs of the pulpit so much (remember, this is your old friend, the fool, talking), let me try my hand at it.
MSG.22: Do they brag of being Hebrews, Israelites, the pure race of Abraham? I'm their match.
MSG.23: Are they servants of Christ? I can go them one better. (I can't believe I'm saying these things. It's crazy to talk this way! But I started, and I'm going to finish.) I've worked much harder, been jailed more often, beaten up more times than I can count, and at death's door time after time.
MSG.24: I've been flogged five times with the Jews' thirty-nine lashes,
MSG.25: beaten by Roman rods three times, pummeled with rocks once. I've been shipwrecked three times, and immersed in the open sea for a night and a day.
MSG.26: In hard traveling year in and year out, I've had to ford rivers, fend off robbers, struggle with friends, struggle with foes. I've been at risk in the city, at risk in the country, endangered by desert sun and sea storm, and betrayed by those I thought were my brothers.
MSG.27: I've known drudgery and hard labor, many a long and lonely night without sleep, many a missed meal, blasted by the cold, naked to the weather.
MSG.28: And that's not the half of it, when you throw in the daily pressures and anxieties of all the churches.
MSG.29: When someone gets to the end of his rope, I feel the desperation in my bones. When someone is duped into sin, an angry fire burns in my gut.
MSG.30: If I have to "brag" about myself, I'll brag about the humiliations that make me like Jesus.
MSG.31: The eternal and blessed God and Father of our Master Jesus knows I'm not lying.
MSG.32: Remember the time I was in Damascus and the governor of King Aretas posted guards at the city gates to arrest me?
MSG.33: I crawled through a window in the wall, was let down in a basket, and had to run for my life.

NIV.1:
Paul and the False Apostles
I hope you will put up with a little of my foolishness; but you are already doing that.
NIV.2: I am jealous for you with a godly jealousy. I promised you to one husband, to Christ, so that I might present you as a pure virgin to him.
NIV.3: But I am afraid that just as Eve was deceived by the serpent’s cunning, your minds may somehow be led astray from your sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
NIV.4: For if someone comes to you and preaches a Jesus other than the Jesus we preached, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or a different gospel from the one you accepted, you put up with it easily enough.
NIV.5: But I do not think I am in the least inferior to those “super-apostles.”
NIV.6: I may not be a trained speaker, but I do have knowledge. We have made this perfectly clear to you in every way.
NIV.7: Was it a sin for me to lower myself in order to elevate you by preaching the gospel of God to you free of charge?
NIV.8: I robbed other churches by receiving support from them so as to serve you.
NIV.9: And when I was with you and needed something, I was not a burden to anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia supplied what I needed. I have kept myself from being a burden to you in any way, and will continue to do so.
NIV.10: As surely as the truth of Christ is in me, nobody in the regions of Achaia will stop this boasting of mine.
NIV.11: Why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
NIV.12: And I will keep on doing what I am doing in order to cut the ground from under those who want an opportunity to be considered equal with us in the things they boast about.
NIV.13: For such men are false apostles, deceitful workmen, masquerading as apostles of Christ.
NIV.14: And no wonder, for Satan himself masquerades as an angel of light.
NIV.15: It is not surprising, then, if his servants masquerade as servants of righteousness. Their end will be what their actions deserve.
NIV.16:
Paul Boasts About His Sufferings
I repeat: Let no one take me for a fool. But if you do, then receive me just as you would a fool, so that I may do a little boasting.
NIV.17: In this self-confident boasting I am not talking as the Lord would, but as a fool.
NIV.18: Since many are boasting in the way the world does, I too will boast.
NIV.19: You gladly put up with fools since you are so wise!
NIV.20: In fact, you even put up with anyone who enslaves you or exploits you or takes advantage of you or pushes himself forward or slaps you in the face.
NIV.21:
To my shame I admit that we were too weak for that!
What anyone else dares to boast about—I am speaking as a fool—I also dare to boast about.
NIV.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they Abraham’s descendants? So am I.
NIV.23: Are they servants of Christ? (I am out of my mind to talk like this.) I am more. I have worked much harder, been in prison more frequently, been flogged more severely, and been exposed to death again and again.
NIV.24: Five times I received from the Jews the forty lashes minus one.
NIV.25: Three times I was beaten with rods, once I was stoned, three times I was shipwrecked, I spent a night and a day in the open sea,
NIV.26: I have been constantly on the move. I have been in danger from rivers, in danger from bandits, in danger from my own countrymen, in danger from Gentiles; in danger in the city, in danger in the country, in danger at sea; and in danger from false brothers.
NIV.27: I have labored and toiled and have often gone without sleep; I have known hunger and thirst and have often gone without food; I have been cold and naked.
NIV.28: Besides everything else, I face daily the pressure of my concern for all the churches.
NIV.29: Who is weak, and I do not feel weak? Who is led into sin, and I do not inwardly burn?
NIV.30: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
NIV.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, who is to be praised forever, knows that I am not lying.
NIV.32: In Damascus the governor under King Aretas had the city of the Damascenes guarded in order to arrest me.
NIV.33: But I was lowered in a basket from a window in the wall and slipped through his hands.

NRSVCE.1: I wish you would bear with me in a little foolishness. Do bear with me!
NRSVCE.2: I feel a divine jealousy for you, for I promised you in marriage to one husband, to present you as a chaste virgin to Christ.
NRSVCE.3: But I am afraid that as the serpent deceived Eve by its cunning, your thoughts will be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
NRSVCE.4: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus than the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit from the one you received, or a different gospel from the one you accepted, you submit to it readily enough.
NRSVCE.5: I think that I am not in the least inferior to these super-apostles.
NRSVCE.6: I may be untrained in speech, but not in knowledge; certainly in every way and in all things we have made this evident to you.
NRSVCE.7: Did I commit a sin by humbling myself so that you might be exalted, because I proclaimed God’s good news to you free of charge?
NRSVCE.8: I robbed other churches by accepting support from them in order to serve you.
NRSVCE.9: And when I was with you and was in need, I did not burden anyone, for my needs were supplied by the friends who came from Macedonia. So I refrained and will continue to refrain from burdening you in any way.
NRSVCE.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boast of mine will not be silenced in the regions of Achaia.
NRSVCE.11: And why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
NRSVCE.12: And what I do I will also continue to do, in order to deny an opportunity to those who want an opportunity to be recognized as our equals in what they boast about.
NRSVCE.13: For such boasters are false apostles, deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
NRSVCE.14: And no wonder! Even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
NRSVCE.15: So it is not strange if his ministers also disguise themselves as ministers of righteousness. Their end will match their deeds.
NRSVCE.16: I repeat, let no one think that I am a fool; but if you do, then accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
NRSVCE.17: What I am saying in regard to this boastful confidence, I am saying not with the Lord’s authority, but as a fool;
NRSVCE.18: since many boast according to human standards, I will also boast.
NRSVCE.19: For you gladly put up with fools, being wise yourselves!
NRSVCE.20: For you put up with it when someone makes slaves of you, or preys upon you, or takes advantage of you, or puts on airs, or gives you a slap in the face.
NRSVCE.21: To my shame, I must say, we were too weak for that! But whatever anyone dares to boast of — I am speaking as a fool — I also dare to boast of that.
NRSVCE.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
NRSVCE.23: Are they ministers of Christ? I am talking like a madman — I am a better one: with far greater labors, far more imprisonments, with countless floggings, and often near death.
NRSVCE.24: Five times I have received from the Jews the forty lashes minus one.
NRSVCE.25: Three times I was beaten with rods. Once I received a stoning. Three times I was shipwrecked; for a night and a day I was adrift at sea;
NRSVCE.26: on frequent journeys, in danger from rivers, danger from bandits, danger from my own people, danger from Gentiles, danger in the city, danger in the wilderness, danger at sea, danger from false brothers and sisters;
NRSVCE.27: in toil and hardship, through many a sleepless night, hungry and thirsty, often without food, cold and naked.
NRSVCE.28: And, besides other things, I am under daily pressure because of my anxiety for all the churches.
NRSVCE.29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is made to stumble, and I am not indignant?
NRSVCE.30: If I must boast, I will boast of the things that show my weakness.
NRSVCE.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus (blessed be he forever!) knows that I do not lie.
NRSVCE.32: In Damascus, the governor under King Aretas guarded the city of Damascus in order to seize me,
NRSVCE.33: but I was let down in a basket through a window in the wall, and escaped from his hands.

NET.1: I wish that you would be patient with me in a little foolishness, but indeed you are being patient with me!
NET.2: For I am jealous for you with godly jealousy, because I promised you in marriage to one husband, to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
NET.3: But I am afraid that just as the serpent deceived Eve by his treachery, your minds may be led astray from a sincere and pure devotion to Christ.
NET.4: For if someone comes and proclaims another Jesus different from the one we proclaimed, or if you receive a different spirit than the one you received, or a different gospel than the one you accepted, you put up with it well enough!
NET.5: For I consider myself not at all inferior to those “super-apostles.”
NET.6: And even if I am unskilled in speaking, yet I am certainly not so in knowledge. Indeed, we have made this plain to you in everything in every way.
NET.7: Or did I commit a sin by humbling myself so that you could be exalted, because I proclaimed the gospel of God to you free of charge?
NET.8: I robbed other churches by receiving support from them so that I could serve you!
NET.9: When I was with you and was in need, I was not a burden to anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia fully supplied my needs. I kept myself from being a burden to you in any way, and will continue to do so.
NET.10: As the truth of Christ is in me, this boasting of mine will not be stopped in the regions of Achaia.
NET.11: Why? Because I do not love you? God knows I do!
NET.12: And what I am doing I will continue to do, so that I may eliminate any opportunity for those who want a chance to be regarded as our equals in the things they boast about.
NET.13: For such people are false apostles, deceitful workers, disguising themselves as apostles of Christ.
NET.14: And no wonder, for even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light.
NET.15: Therefore it is not surprising his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness, whose end will correspond to their actions.
NET.16: I say again, let no one think that I am a fool. But if you do, then at least accept me as a fool, so that I too may boast a little.
NET.17: What I am saying with this boastful confidence I do not say the way the Lord would. Instead it is, as it were, foolishness.
NET.18: Since many are boasting according to human standards, I too will boast.
NET.19: For since you are so wise, you put up with fools gladly.
NET.20: For you put up with it if someone makes slaves of you, if someone exploits you, if someone takes advantage of you, if someone behaves arrogantly toward you, if someone strikes you in the face.
NET.21: (To my disgrace I must say that we were too weak for that!) But whatever anyone else dares to boast about (I am speaking foolishly), I also dare to boast about the same thing.
NET.22: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
NET.23: Are they servants of Christ? (I am talking like I am out of my mind!) I am even more so: with much greater labors, with far more imprisonments, with more severe beatings, facing death many times.
NET.24: Five times I received from the Jews forty lashes less one.
NET.25: Three times I was beaten with a rod. Once I received a stoning. Three times I suffered shipwreck. A night and a day I spent adrift in the open sea.
NET.26: I have been on journeys many times, in dangers from rivers, in dangers from robbers, in dangers from my own countrymen, in dangers from Gentiles, in dangers in the city, in dangers in the wilderness, in dangers at sea, in dangers from false brothers,
NET.27: in hard work and toil, through many sleepless nights, in hunger and thirst, many times without food, in cold and without enough clothing.
NET.28: Apart from other things, there is the daily pressure on me of my anxious concern for all the churches.
NET.29: Who is weak, and I am not weak? Who is led into sin, and I do not burn with indignation?
NET.30: If I must boast, I will boast about the things that show my weakness.
NET.31: The God and Father of the Lord Jesus, who is blessed forever, knows I am not lying.
NET.32: In Damascus, the governor under King Aretas was guarding the city of Damascus in order to arrest me,
NET.33: but I was let down in a rope-basket through a window in the city wall, and escaped his hands.
